mechanism_narrative: Persistently high inflation coupled with a restrictive federal funds rate signals the central bank will maintain tight financial conditions, increasing the discount rate for equities and suppressing forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects broad-based economic strength and supports corporate earnings growth, leading to positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The 6-month change in PAYEMS has a rolling 3-year rank correlation with 60-day forward returns that is less than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts indicates a severe slowdown in a key interest-rate sensitive sector, foreshadowing broader economic weakness and negative equity market performance.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing is a leading indicator of the business cycle.
failure_condition: In periods following a >15% 6-month drop in HOUST, 60-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects robust manufacturing activity and corporate capital investment, which translates to higher expected earnings and positive short-term equity performance.
failure_condition: The 3-month rate of change of INDPRO fails to show a statistically significant positive correlation (p > 0.10) with 20-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate combined with stalling payroll growth provides a dual signal of a deteriorating labor market, increasing recession probability and leading to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3mo avg > 12mo avg AND PAYEMS 3mo change < 100
evidence_refs: fact:The Sahm Rule uses rising unemployment to signal recessions.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 posts positive 60-day returns in over 50% of instances where the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: In a high interest rate environment, any further increase in the policy rate has a disproportionately negative impact on equity valuations due to higher discount rates and tighter financial conditions.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases in the latest month
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 4%, monthly increases in the rate are not followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns on average over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Decelerating real retail sales growth indicates weakening consumer demand, which is a primary driver of corporate revenues. This slowdown leads to downward earnings revisions and negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending accounts for ~70% of US GDP.
failure_condition: The 6-month rate of change in RSAFS has a rank correlation with 20-day forward returns that is not statistically different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth occurring simultaneously with a high federal funds rate creates a 'good news is bad news' scenario. The market anticipates the Fed must tighten further to cool the economy, leading to negative forward returns.
failure_condition: In periods where both trigger conditions are met, the S&P 500 has positive 20-day forward returns more than 50% of the time.
